tips.tipText: Swap sugary sodas for sparkling water with a slice of lemon or lime. It's refreshing and has zero sugar.
Feeling tired? A 10-minute walk can boost your energy for up to two hours.
Instead of checking your phone first thing in the morning, try 5 minutes of mindful stretching.
The '2-minute rule': If a task takes less than two minutes to complete, do it immediately.
Keep a bottle of water on your desk. Seeing it will remind you to stay hydrated throughout the day.
Feeling stressed? Try the 4-7-8 breathing technique: Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, and exhale for 8.
Add one colorful vegetable to your dinner plate each night.
Schedule 'worry time'. Dedicate 15 minutes each day to acknowledge your anxieties, then let them go.
Park a little further from the entrance when you go shopping to get some extra steps in.
An hour before bed, put your phone away. The blue light can interfere with your sleep quality.
Eat your meals without distractions. Turn off the TV and put your phone away to savor your food.
Feeling overwhelmed by a big project? Break it down into small, 15-minute tasks.
Take the stairs instead of the elevator whenever possible. It's a mini-workout for your heart.
Practice gratitude. Before you go to sleep, think of three things you were grateful for today.
Swap out processed snacks like chips for a handful of almonds or an apple.
If you sit at a desk, stand up and stretch for one minute every hour.
Create a 'get ready for bed' routine, like reading a chapter of a book or listening to calm music.
Tidy up your workspace for 5 minutes at the end of each day. You'll start the next day fresh.
Green tea contains L-theanine, which provides a calmer, more focused energy boost than coffee jitters.
On weekends, spend at least 30 minutes in nature. A walk in a park can significantly reduce stress.
Need a sweet treat? Try a piece of dark chocolate (70% cacao or higher) instead of a candy bar.
Don't skip breakfast. A protein-rich meal can prevent cravings and energy slumps later in the day.
Feeling uninspired? Change your environment. Work from a different room or a local coffee shop for an hour.
Follow the 20-20-20 rule to reduce eye strain: Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
Make your bed every morning. It's a small win that starts your day with a sense of accomplishment.
When you feel an urge for a bad habit, drink a full glass of water first. The craving might pass.
Listen to a podcast or audiobook during your commute or while doing chores.
Instead of a second cup of coffee, try a cup of peppermint tea to boost focus.
Write down your top 3 priorities for the next day before you finish work.
Once a week, try a new healthy recipe. It makes healthy eating more exciting.
Do a 'brain dump'. If your mind is racing, write down everything you're thinking about on a piece of paper.
If you work from home, take a 'fake commute' walk around the block before and after your workday.
Set a timer for 25 minutes and work on a single task without interruption (the Pomodoro Technique).
Swap mayonnaise on your sandwich for hummus or avocado.
Unfollow social media accounts that make you feel bad about yourself.
Can't fall asleep? Get out of bed and do a relaxing activity like reading until you feel sleepy.
Prepare your clothes and lunch for the next day before you go to bed. Your future self will thank you.
When you have a craving, wait 10 minutes. It might just be boredom, not actual hunger.
Smile at a stranger. It's a small act of kindness that can boost your mood and theirs.
Dedicate the first hour of your workday to your most important task, before checking emails.
Swap fruit juice, which is high in sugar, for a whole piece of fruit, which has fiber.
Feeling stiff? Do a few simple neck rolls and shoulder shrugs at your desk.
Plan your meals for the week. It saves time, money, and reduces the stress of deciding what to eat.
If a negative thought enters your mind, challenge it. Ask yourself, 'Is this thought actually true?'
Turn off push notifications for non-essential apps on your phone to reduce distractions.
Try to eat at least one meal a day with someone else. Social connection is a key part of well-being.
Keep your bedroom cool, dark, and quiet for optimal sleep.
Replace white rice and bread with whole-grain alternatives like brown rice or quinoa.
If you're feeling anxious, try naming 5 things you can see, 4 things you can touch, 3 things you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste.
End your shower with a 30-second blast of cold water to feel awake and energized.
